646	  Mathlib hooks the prover will assemble for the formalised version of this
647	  block at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (i) \texttt{Algebra.FormallySmooth} and the
648	  smooth-locus-of-a-scheme typeclass instance threading
649	  \texttt{[Smooth\,X.hom]} down to the affine algebra
650	  \(\bar k \to \mathcal O_X(U)\) on each open affine \(U \subseteq X\); (ii) the
651	  Jacobian-criterion refinement of \texttt{Module.FormallyEtale} /
652	  \texttt{Algebra.Smooth} via the cotangent complex \texttt{H\^{}1\,LSm} (or
653	  equivalent), as encoded in Mathlib's \texttt{Algebra.Smooth.iff} family; (iii)
654	  the closed-point selection that, given a non-closed \(z\), picks a closed
655	  specialisation \(x \rightsquigarrow z\) whose stalk localises to
656	  \(\mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.localRing\_at\_closed\_point}
657	  family); (iv) preservation of regularity under localisation of a regular
658	  local ring at a prime (Mathlib
659	  \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.localization\_isRegularLocalRing}, or the same
660	  fact phrased via Stacks tag \texttt{00OF}). The composition of (i)--(iv) is
661	  the formalised content of the smooth-implies-regular implication; the only
662	  Mathlib gap at \texttt{b80f227} is in step (ii)--(iii), specifically the
663	  Jacobian-criterion scheme-level packaging that promotes
664	  \texttt{[Smooth\,X.hom]\,[IsAlgClosed\,\bar k]} to
665	  \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing\,(X.left.presheaf.stalk\,z)}.
666	
667	  As a backup citation, Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Chapter~19
668	  (``Regular Rings'') develops the regular-local-ring theory needed for
669	  step~(iv); see \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} for the depth-/CM-side
670	  references the project already maintains.
671	\end{proof}

676	  [Smooth \(\Rightarrow\) DVR at every codim-\(1\) point]
677	  \label{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}
678	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one}
679	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, p.~130 (the definition ``regular in codimension one'',
680	  % together with the immediately preceding remark that on a nonsingular variety every
681	  % local ring is regular) (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf,
682	  % PDF page 147).
683	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textbf{Definition.} We say a scheme \(X\) is \emph{regular in
684	  % codimension one} (or sometimes \emph{nonsingular in codimension one}) if every
685	  % local ring \(\mathcal O_x\) of \(X\) of dimension one is regular."
686	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The most important examples of such schemes are nonsingular
687	  % varieties over a field (I, \S5) and noetherian normal schemes. On a nonsingular
688	  % variety the local ring of every closed point is regular (I, 5.1), hence all the
689	  % local rings are regular, since they are localizations of the local rings of
690	  % closed points."
691	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
692	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
693	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\)."
694	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~130 (definition of regular in codimension one;
695	  the DVR consequence at a codim-1 point).}
696	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\) and let \(\eta \in X\) be a point with
697	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a
698	  discrete valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\) of \(X\).
699	\end{lemma}
700	
701	\begin{proof}
702	
703	  % NOTE (iter-185 writer): the `` marker on this proof block reflects
704	  % the Krull-dim half (Tier-1 axiom-clean closure achieved iter-184 via
705	  % `Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` from the iter-183
706	  % `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`, see
707	  % `\cref{chap:Albanese_CoheightBridge}`). The `IsRegularLocalRing` half is
708	  % tracked separately as the Stacks 00TT gap and is now packaged in
709	  % `\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}` above; that block carries no
710	  % `` because the Mathlib hooks (i)--(iv) in its proof sketch are not
711	  % all axiom-clean at commit `b80f227`. The composition (00TT regularity +
712	  % iter-183 Krull-dim bridge) inside the private Lean helper
713	  % `smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`
714	  % (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:223`) keeps a single
715	  % typed `sorry` on the regularity half pending an iter-185+ prover round.
716	  By \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} (Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}, the
717	  Jacobian-criterion direction), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular for
718	  every point \(x \in X\) --- in particular at every closed point (the case
719	  Hartshorne~I.5.1 states classically). Localising a regular local ring at a
720	  prime ideal yields a regular local ring; in particular, for \(\eta \in X\) with
721	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\), the local ring
722	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is regular of Krull dimension \(1\). A regular local ring of Krull dimension \(1\) is exactly a
723	  discrete valuation ring (Atiyah--Macdonald, Proposition 9.2; or Stacks tag
724	  \texttt{00PD}). Its fraction field is the function field \(K(X)\) of \(X\) by
725	  irreducibility of \(X\) (the unique generic point of \(X\) lies above \(\eta\), and the
726	  stalk at the generic point of an integral scheme is the function field).
727	
728	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
729	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} is the
730	  instance/lemma stating that \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a Mathlib
731	  \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing} under the typeclass set
732	  \texttt{[Smooth\,(\dots)\,X.hom]\ [IsIntegral X.left]} and the hypothesis
733	  \texttt{Order.coheight\ \(\eta\) = 1}. The fraction-field identification with
734	  \texttt{X.functionField} is a downstream named instance
735	  \texttt{Scheme.fractionRing\_localRing\_eq\_functionField} (or whatever Mathlib name
736	  the prover chooses at commit \texttt{b80f227}). The regular-local-ring-of-dimension-1
737	  \(\Leftrightarrow\) DVR equivalence is shipped in Mathlib as
738	  \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.isDiscreteValuationRing\_of\_dim\_one} (or analogous);
739	  the prover re-exports it rather than re-proving.
740	\end{proof}

747	point.

23	   corresponding prime `𝔭_Y ⊂ R` is a height-1 prime. The
24	   height-1 primes containing `f` are precisely the minimal primes
25	   of `(f) ⊂ R`, which is a finite set

27	   / Krull's Hauptidealsatz).

29	   of `X`. Under `[IsNoetherian X]` (= locally Noeth + compact),
30	   `X` is Noetherian as a topological space (Stacks 04ZE), so
31	   `X \ U` has finitely many irreducible components. Each codim-1
32	   component contributes at most one nonzero order; bound by the
33	   `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` hypothesis on `X`.
34	5. Sum of finite + finite = finite.

1	# Analogy: Closed-point cotangent iso (Stacks 02JK)
2	
3	## Mode
4	cross-domain-inspiration
5	
6	## Slug
7	coe-stacks02jk
8	
9	## Iteration
10	199
11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	
14	We have a commutative diagram of `R`-algebras `R → A → B` with `A → B`
15	surjective with kernel `J`. Mathlib gives us a *right-exact* sequence
16	`J.Cotangent →[kerCotangentToTensor] B ⊗_A Ω[A/R] →[mapBaseChange] Ω[B/R] → 0`.
17	We want this to be a short exact sequence (with the leftmost map injective)
18	AND, when the rightmost term vanishes, an isomorphism on the left side.
19	The injectivity step requires a *retraction* (a left-inverse on the
20	cotangent complex), which is precisely the algebraic content of "`B` is
21	formally smooth over `R`".
22	
23	In our concrete setting: `A = S_m` (a smooth-algebra localization at a
24	closed point), `B = κ` (the residue field of `S_m`, equal to the ground
25	field `R = k̄` because the point is `k̄`-rational), `J = m`. Then
26	`J.Cotangent = m/m² = IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace S_m`, and we want the
27	iso `m/m² ≃ κ ⊗_{S_m} Ω[S_m/R]`.
28	
29	## Failed approaches (from directive)
30	
31	- **Direct `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`**: gives the right
32	  half of the conormal sequence (surjectivity onto `κ ⊗ Ω` modulo
33	  `Ω[κ/k]`), but NOT injectivity of the leftmost map.
34	- **Conormal sequence + `Ω[κ/k] = 0` alone**: cannot establish the left
35	  end of the short exact sequence without an extra splitting/injectivity
36	  argument.
37	
38	The common missing ingredient in both failed approaches is the
39	*retraction* — a left-inverse for the cotangent map. Mathlib supplies it
40	under the name "formally smooth" via several distinct API surfaces; the
41	project's failed approaches simply did not invoke any of them.
42	
43	## Analogues found
44	
45	### Analogue 1: `Algebra.Extension.formallySmooth_iff_split_injection`
46	
47	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / Kähler differential theory.
48	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`.
49	- **Same structural problem there**: given an extension
50	  `P : Algebra.Extension R A` (so `P.Ring → A` surjective with kernel
51	  `P.ker`), under the hypothesis `Algebra.FormallySmooth R P.Ring`, the
52	  statement
53	    `Algebra.FormallySmooth R A ↔ ∃ l, l ∘ₗ P.cotangentComplex = LinearMap.id`
54	  reformulates "A is FS over R" as the *existence of a retraction* on the
55	  cotangent complex `P.Cotangent →ₗ[A] P.CotangentSpace`. The cotangent
56	  complex is the abstract analogue of "J/J² → B ⊗_A Ω[A/R]".
57	- **Technique**: bundle the surjection `A → B` as an `Algebra.Extension R B`,
58	  obtain the retraction `l` from the formally-smooth hypothesis on `B`
59	  (not on `A`!), and immediately conclude that `P.cotangentComplex` is
60	  injective via `Function.LeftInverse.injective`.
61	- **Mapping to project**: instantiate
62	  `P := Algebra.Extension.ofSurjective (IsLocalRing.residue S_m).toAlgHom (...)`
63	  with `R = k̄`, `A = κ = IsLocalRing.ResidueField S_m`. Then `P.Ring = S_m`
64	  (formally smooth over R because *smooth* over R via Stage 4 substrate),
65	  `P.ker = maximalIdeal S_m`, `P.Cotangent ≃ (maximalIdeal S_m).Cotangent`
66	  (bridged via `Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.of` / `.val`), and
67	  `P.CotangentSpace = κ ⊗_{S_m} Ω[S_m/R]`. The hypothesis
68	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth R κ` is automatic for `κ = k̄` algebraically
69	  closed at a `k̄`-rational closed point (it's the identity). Conclude:
70	  `P.cotangentComplex` injective. Combine with
71	  `Algebra.Extension.exact_cotangentComplex_toKaehler` (exact at middle)
72	  and `Subsingleton Ω[κ/R]` (from `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective`
73	  on `R → κ`) to upgrade injective+exact-with-zero-target to bijective.
74	  Finalize via `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`.
75	- **Porting cost**: low. ~60–90 LOC of typeclass scaffolding +
76	  application. No new Mathlib lemma needed.
77	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
78	
79	### Analogue 2: `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`
80	
81	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / Kähler differential theory.
82	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`.
83	- **Same structural problem there**: the un-bundled form of Analogue 1.
84	  For `R → P → A` with `P → A` surjective and `P` formally smooth over `R`,
85	    `Algebra.FormallySmooth R A ↔ ∃ l, l ∘ₗ KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R P A = LinearMap.id`
86	  where `kerCotangentToTensor R P A : (ker(P → A)).Cotangent → A ⊗_P Ω[P/R]`
87	  is exactly the map we want to invert.
88	- **Technique**: same retraction trick, but stated directly on Mathlib's
89	  un-bundled `kerCotangentToTensor` rather than the
90	  `Algebra.Extension.cotangentComplex` packaging.
91	- **Mapping to project**: set `R = k̄`, `P = S_m`, `A = κ`. The map
92	  `kerCotangentToTensor R S_m κ : m.Cotangent → κ ⊗_{S_m} Ω[S_m/R]`
93	  is what we want bijective. The codomain identification is essentially
94	  definitional (no Algebra.Extension.Cotangent bridge needed). The
95	  domain `m.Cotangent` already matches `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace S_m`
96	  by abbreviation. After the FS hypothesis on `κ/R` provides the
97	  retraction, combine with `exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange`
98	  (exact at middle, conditional on `S_m → κ` surjective — automatic)
99	  and `Subsingleton Ω[κ/R]` to get bijectivity. `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`
100	  for the iso.
101	- **Porting cost**: low. ~40–70 LOC, simpler than Analogue 1 because it
102	  avoids the `Algebra.Extension.Cotangent` ↔ `Ideal.Cotangent` bridge.
103	  The result is `S_m`-linear; if the project wants `κ`-linear, lift via
104	  the `IsLocalRing.instModuleResidueFieldCotangentSpace` /
105	  `IsScalarTower` instances.
106	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND. **Top recommendation.**
107	
108	### Analogue 3: `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_injective_lTensor_residueField`
109	
110	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / Kähler differential theory.
111	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Local`.
112	- **Same structural problem there**: for `S` a local `R`-algebra and an
113	  extension `P : Algebra.Extension R S`, under appropriate freeness +
114	  finiteness + FG hypotheses,
115	    `Algebra.FormallySmooth R S ↔ Function.Injective (LinearMap.lTensor κ(S) P.cotangentComplex)`
116	  where the LHS asks injectivity AFTER tensoring with the residue field
117	  of `S` (which is the additional ingredient compared to Analogue 1).
118	- **Technique**: same retraction-implies-injection technique, but the
119	  conclusion is stated post-tensoring with `κ(S)`. Useful when one wants
120	  to assert injectivity on the residue-field-base-changed object directly.
121	- **Mapping to project**: more natural fit when `S` is the smooth-algebra
122	  localization itself (the LHS local ring), and we want a statement about
123	  `κ(S) ⊗_{S_m} P.cotangentComplex`. But for our problem, the target ring
124	  is the residue field `κ`, not `S_m`, so this analogue is structurally
125	  one indirection further away from what we need. Still applicable: set
126	  `S = κ`, `P.Ring = S_m`. Tensoring with `κ(κ) = κ` is trivial.
127	- **Porting cost**: medium. Heavier typeclass scaffolding
128	  (`Module.Free P.Ring Ω[P.Ring/R]`, `Module.Finite ...`, `P.ker.FG`),
129	  plus a no-op residue-field tensor at the end. ~80–120 LOC.
130	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND (but redundant given Analogues 1–2).
131	
132	### Analogue 4: `Function.Exact.linearEquivOfSurjective` (structural assembly helper)
133	
134	- **Domain**: pure module / linear algebra. `Mathlib.Algebra.Exact`.
135	- **Same structural problem there**: given `Function.Exact f g` and
136	  `g` surjective, produces `N ⧸ f.range ≃ P`. Standard exact-sequence
137	  bookkeeping with no ring-theoretic content.
138	- **Technique**: snake-lemma-style assembly: exactness in the middle +
139	  surjectivity at the right gives `(middle / range_of_left) ≃ right`;
140	  when `right = 0`, this collapses to surjectivity of the left map.
141	- **Mapping to project**: useful as the *final assembly step* in
142	  Analogues 1–3. Once injectivity + exactness + `Ω[κ/R] = 0` are in
143	  hand, this lemma (or `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`) extracts the iso.
144	- **Porting cost**: trivial (already a one-liner in Mathlib).
145	- **Verdict**: PARTIAL_ANALOGUE — supporting helper, not a structural
146	  technique on its own.
147	
148	### Analogue 5: `Algebra.Generators.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
149	
150	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / generator-based Kähler theory.
151	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Free`.
152	- **Same structural problem there**: for a presentation `P` with `Ω[S/R]`
153	  having a basis indexed by a subset of generators (which is the case for
154	  standard-smooth presentations), the cotangent restriction
155	  `P.cotangentRestrict : P.toExtension.Cotangent → (σ →₀ S)` is bijective
156	  under a `Subsingleton (H¹Cotangent R S)` hypothesis.
157	- **Technique**: directly compute `P.Cotangent ≃ (σ →₀ S)` via the
158	  combinatorial structure of the presentation, then identify the RHS with
159	  `S ⊗_P Ω[P/R]` via `KaehlerDifferential.mvPolynomialEquiv`.
160	- **Mapping to project**: applicable to the polynomial-ring presentation
161	  of `S = R[x_1, …, x_n] / I` (the standard-smooth presentation), but not
162	  directly to the local ring `S_m → κ`. To use it for the residue-field
163	  iso we'd need to chain `S → S_m → κ` and base-change the Cotangent
164	  identification. Significantly more work than Analogues 1–3.
165	- **Porting cost**: high. Goes through `Algebra.Generators` API which the
166	  project does not yet wire up (Stage 4 substrate uses `IsStandardSmooth`,
167	  not `Generators` directly). ~150–250 LOC.
168	- **Verdict**: PARTIAL_ANALOGUE — feasible but heavier than Analogues 1–2.
169	
170	## Top suggestion
171	
172	Try **Analogue 2** first: `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` in
173	`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`. The proof shape:
174	
175	```lean
176	-- inputs: R = kbar, P = S_m, A = κ = ResidueField S_m
177	-- have: Algebra.FormallySmooth R S_m   (from Stage-4 smooth ⟹ FS)
178	-- have: Algebra.FormallySmooth R κ      (κ = R algebraically closed)
179	-- have: Function.Surjective (algebraMap S_m κ)   (residue is surjective)
180	
181	-- Step 1: retraction → injection
182	obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection hSurj).mp ‹_›
183	have hInj : Function.Injective (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R S_m κ) :=
184	  Function.LeftInverse.injective (fun x => congr_arg _ (LinearMap.congr_fun hl x))
185	
186	-- Step 2: Ω[κ/R] = 0 + exactness → surjection
187	have hOmegaZero : Subsingleton (Ω[κ⁄R]) :=
188	  KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective R κ Function.Surjective.id   -- κ = R
189	have hExact := KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R S_m κ hSurj
190	-- range(kerCotangentToTensor) = ker(mapBaseChange) = whole tensor space (target is 0)
191	
192	-- Step 3: bijective → iso
193	exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurj_kct⟩
194	```
195	
196	The first file to touch is
197	`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` around line 615
198	(where the iso is currently expressed as the unresolved sub-gap (ii.A)),
199	to fold in this iso as a fresh private theorem and consume it in
200	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` via `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`.
201	
202	Estimated total LOC: 40–70, well below the directive's 100–200
203	estimate, because the retraction is supplied for free by Mathlib's
204	formally-smooth iff-lemma rather than constructed by hand.
205	
206	If Analogue 2 hits an unforeseen scalar-tower / coercion issue (the
207	`κ`-linearity upgrade from `S_m`-linear is the most likely friction
208	point), fall back to **Analogue 1** (`Algebra.Extension`-packaged form),
209	which carries the residue-field module structure more cleanly through
210	`Algebra.Extension.CotangentSpace`'s built-in `S`-module structure.
211	
212	## Discarded
213	
214	- **`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` alone** — matches
215	  failed approach (1).
216	- **Conormal-only approach without retraction** — matches failed
217	  approach (2).
218	- **`Algebra.Generators.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential`**
219	  — feasible but heavier (Analogue 5), worth keeping in reserve only if
220	  Analogues 1–3 fail.

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Quotient.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.MaximalIdeal.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	
13	# Residue Field of local rings
14	
15	## Main definitions
16	
17	* `IsLocalRing.ResidueField`: The quotient of a local ring by its maximal ideal.
18	* `IsLocalRing.residue`: The quotient map from a local ring to its residue field.
19	-/
20	
21	@[expose] public section
22	
23	namespace IsLocalRing
24	
25	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
26	
27	/-- The residue field of a local ring is the quotient of the ring by its maximal ideal. -/
28	def ResidueField :=
29	  R ⧸ maximalIdeal R
30	deriving CommRing, Inhabited
31	
32	noncomputable instance ResidueField.field : Field (ResidueField R) :=
33	  fast_instance% Ideal.Quotient.field (maximalIdeal R)
34	
35	/-- The quotient map from a local ring to its residue field. -/
36	def residue : R →+* ResidueField R :=
37	  Ideal.Quotient.mk _
38	
39	end IsLocalRing

13	
14	/-!
15	
16	# Residue Field of local rings
17	
18	We prove basic properties of the residue field of a local ring.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	variable {R S T : Type*}
25	
26	namespace IsLocalRing
27	
28	section
29	
30	variable [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [CommRing S] [IsLocalRing S] [CommRing T] [IsLocalRing T]
31	
32	lemma residue_def (x) : residue R x = Ideal.Quotient.mk (maximalIdeal R) x := rfl
33	
34	lemma ker_residue : RingHom.ker (residue R) = maximalIdeal R :=
35	  Ideal.mk_ker
36	
37	@[simp]
38	lemma residue_eq_zero_iff (x : R) : residue R x = 0 ↔ x ∈ maximalIdeal R := by
39	  rw [← RingHom.mem_ker, ker_residue]
40	
41	lemma residue_ne_zero_iff_isUnit (x : R) : residue R x ≠ 0 ↔ IsUnit x := by
42	  simp
43	
44	lemma residue_surjective :
45	    Function.Surjective (IsLocalRing.residue R) :=
46	  Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
47	
48	variable (R)
49	
50	instance ResidueField.algebra {R₀} [CommRing R₀] [Algebra R₀ R] :
51	    Algebra R₀ (ResidueField R) :=
52	  inferInstanceAs <| Algebra R₀ (_ ⧸ _)
53	
54	instance {R₁ R₂} [CommRing R₁] [CommRing R₂]
55	    [Algebra R₁ R₂] [Algebra R₁ R] [Algebra R₂ R] [IsScalarTower R₁ R₂ R] :
56	    IsScalarTower R₁ R₂ (ResidueField R) :=
57	  inferInstanceAs <| IsScalarTower R₁ R₂ (_ ⧸ _)
58	
59	@[simp]
60	theorem ResidueField.algebraMap_eq : algebraMap R (ResidueField R) = residue R :=
61	  rfl

import Mathlib

open KaehlerDifferential

noncomputable example {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ ⁄ R])] :
    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via iff_split_injection
  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
    (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
      (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
  have hInj : Function.Injective
      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
    simpa using this
  -- Step 2: Ω[κ/R] = 0 + exactness → surjection
  have hExact :=
    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
  have hSurjMap :
      Function.Surjective
        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    intro x
    rw [hExact x]
    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
  -- Step 3: bijective → iso
  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

11	
12	/-! # Exactness of a pair
13	
14	* For two maps `f : M → N` and `g : N → P`, with `Zero P`,
15	  `Function.Exact f g` says that `Set.range f = Set.preimage g {0}`
16	
17	* For two maps `f : M → N` and `g : N → P`, with `One P`,
18	  `Function.MulExact f g` says that `Set.range f = Set.preimage g {1}`
19	
20	* For additive maps `f : M →+ N`  and `g : N →+ P`,
21	  `Exact f g` says that `range f = ker g`
22	
23	* For multiplicative maps `f : M →* N`  and `g : N →* P`,
24	  `MulExact f g` says that `range f = ker g`
25	
26	* For linear maps `f : M →ₗ[R] N`  and `g : N →ₗ[R] P`,
27	  `Exact f g` says that `range f = ker g`
28	
29	## TODO :
30	
31	* generalize to `SemilinearMap`, even `SemilinearMapClass`
32	-/
33

35

41	
42	/-- The maps `f` and `g` form an exact pair: `g y = 1` iff `y` belongs to the image of `f`. -/
43	@[to_additive Exact /-- The maps `f` and `g` form an exact pair:
44	  `g y = 0` iff `y` belongs to the image of `f`. -/]
45	def MulExact [One P] : Prop := ∀ y, g y = 1 ↔ y ∈ Set.range f
46	
47	variable {f g}
48	
49	namespace MulExact
50	
51	@[to_additive]
52	lemma apply_apply_eq_one [One P] (h : MulExact f g) (x : M) :
53	    g (f x) = 1 := (h _).mpr <| Set.mem_range_self _
54	
55	@[to_additive]
56	lemma comp_eq_one [One P] (h : MulExact f g) : g.comp f = 1 :=
57	  funext h.apply_apply_eq_one

61	    (h2 : ∀ x, g x = 1 → x ∈ Set.range f) : MulExact f g :=

import Mathlib

open KaehlerDifferential

noncomputable def myEx {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ ⁄ R])] :
    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via iff_split_injection
  have hInj : Function.Injective
      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
    simpa using this
  -- Step 2: Ω[κ/R] = 0 + exactness → surjection
  have hExact :=
    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
  have hSurjMap :
      Function.Surjective
        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    intro x
    have h0 : KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
      Subsingleton.elim _ _
    exact (hExact x).mp h0
  -- Step 3: bijective → iso
  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

#print axioms myEx

/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (Stacks 02JK, LHS-RHS bridge): formally-smooth residue
gives the cotangent iso.** Iter-199 Lane COE axiom-clean closed-point-style
helper: assuming the source ring `Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, its residue
field `κ = ResidueField Sₘ` is formally smooth over `R`, and `Ω[κ⁄R]` is
subsingleton (the three hypotheses combine to model the closed-point case of
Stacks 02JK over an algebraically closed base, where `κ = R` makes both FS
and `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` automatic), the canonical Mathlib map
`KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ`
is an `Sₘ`-linear equivalence between
`(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (the `Sₘ`-side packaging of `m/m²`)
and `κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` (the residue-field base-change of the Kähler
differentials).

This is the closed-point case of the Stacks-02JK cotangent ↔ Kähler iso
(sub-gap (ii.A) of the Stage 6 chain in `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`).
The proof is the 3-step recipe from `analogies/coe-stacks02jk.md`:

* **Step 1** — retraction → injection via
  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`: under
  `FormallySmooth R κ` and surjectivity of `Sₘ → κ`, the conormal map
  `kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ κ` admits a left-inverse `l`, hence is injective.
* **Step 2** — `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection: combining
  `KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` with
  `Subsingleton (Ω[κ⁄R])` gives that every element of the tensor space lies
  in the kernel of `mapBaseChange`, hence in the range of `kerCotangentToTensor`.
* **Step 3** — bijective → iso: package as `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`.

The `kerCotangentToTensor` map's domain is
`(RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent`, which equals
`(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent = IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ` since
`κ = Sₘ / maximalIdeal Sₘ`; the equivalence is therefore the
Stacks 02JK closed-point cotangent iso
`m/m² ≃ₗ[Sₘ] κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`.

Axiom-clean: composes
`Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` (Mathlib;
`Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/Basic.lean`),
`KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` (Mathlib;
`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean`), and `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`. -/
private noncomputable def
    cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  -- Surjectivity of `algebraMap Sₘ κ` is the residue surjection.
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via `iff_split_injection`.
  have hInj : Function.Injective
      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
    simpa using this
  -- Step 2: `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection.
  have hExact :=
    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
  have hSurjMap :
      Function.Surjective
        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ
          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    intro x
    have h0 :
        KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ
          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
      Subsingleton.elim _ _
    exact (hExact x).mp h0
  -- Step 3: bijective → linear equivalence.
  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

/-- **Stage 6 sub-gap (i) resolution (iter-198).** Axiom-clean substrate helper:

559	def LinearMap.extendScalarsOfSurjectiveEquiv (h : Surjective (algebraMap R S)) :
560	    (M →ₗ[R] N) ≃ₗ[R] (M →ₗ[S] N) where
561	  toFun f := { __ := f, map_smul' := fun r x ↦ by obtain ⟨r, rfl⟩ := h r; simp }

import Mathlib

open KaehlerDifferential

-- Helper iso (we already proved it)
noncomputable def cotangent_iso
    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  have hInj : Function.Injective
      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
    simpa using this
  have hExact :=
    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
  have hSurjMap :
      Function.Surjective
        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ
          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    intro x
    have h0 : KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
      Subsingleton.elim _ _
    exact (hExact x).mp h0
  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

-- Try a finrank corollary
noncomputable example
    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])]
    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
    (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
      (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n := by
  -- finrank κ (κ ⊗ Ω) = n via 6.B-RHS substrate
  have hRHS :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
        (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
    rw [Module.finrank_baseChange]
    exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
  -- The iso (Sₘ-linear) between (ker).Cotangent and κ ⊗ Ω.
  have hiso :
      (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    cotangent_iso
  -- Bridge: RingHom.ker = maximalIdeal.
  have hker : RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) =
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, IsLocalRing.ker_residue]
  -- Substitute in the goal: CotangentSpace Sₘ = (maximalIdeal).Cotangent
  show Module.finrank _ ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent) = n
  -- Transport iso to a κ-linear equiv via extendScalars
  have hiso' :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
    convert hiso using 1
    rw [hker]
  -- Extend scalars to κ
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  have hisoκ :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective hSurj hiso'
  -- Apply finrank_eq
  rw [hisoκ.finrank_eq, hRHS]

import Mathlib

open KaehlerDifferential

noncomputable def cotangent_iso
    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  have hInj : Function.Injective
      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=

import Mathlib

open KaehlerDifferential

noncomputable def cotangent_iso
    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  have hInj : Function.Injective
      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
    simpa using this
  have hExact :=
    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
  have hSurjMap :
      Function.Surjective
        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ
          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    intro x
    have h0 : KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
      Subsingleton.elim _ _
    exact (hExact x).mp h0
  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

noncomputable def cotangent_iso_max
    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
    (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
      TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) Ω[Sₘ⁄R] := by
  have hker : RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) =
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, IsLocalRing.ker_residue]
  rw [← hker]
  exact cotangent_iso

-- The finrank corollary
theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_eq
    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])]
    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
    (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
      (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n := by
  have hRHS :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
        (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n := by
    rw [Module.finrank_baseChange]
    exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
  -- Sₘ-linear iso
  have hiso :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    cotangent_iso_max
  have hSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  -- κ-linear via extendScalars
  have hisoκ :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective hSurj hiso
  rw [hisoκ.finrank_eq, hRHS]

#print axioms finrank_cotangentSpace_eq

private noncomputable def
    cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]

    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]

      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, IsLocalRing.ker_residue]

/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), κ-finrank conclusion.** Assemble the
maximal-ideal-side cotangent iso of
`cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue`
with the 6.B-RHS substrate
`finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq` to compute
the κ-finrank of `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ` (= `m/m²`) explicitly.

Under the closed-point-style hypothesis pattern (`Sₘ` formally smooth over
`R`, residue field `κ` formally smooth over `R`, `Ω[κ⁄R]` subsingleton),
combined with `Module.Free Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` of `Sₘ`-rank `n` (the conclusion of

space `m/m²` has `κ`-finrank equal to `n`.

This is the precise conclusion the consumer
`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` needs: combined with the still-open
Stacks-00OE Krull-dim formula (sub-gap (ii.B)) it discharges the regularity
criterion `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`. Sub-gap (ii.A)

(promotion to κ-linear via the residue surjection), `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`,
and the 6.B-RHS substrate `finrank_baseChange + finrank_eq_of_rank_eq`. -/
private theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
    [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)]
    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])]
    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
    (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
      (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n := by
  -- 6.B-RHS substrate: finrank κ (κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n.
  have hRHS :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
        (TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) = n :=
    finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq n hrank
  -- Sₘ-linear iso (maximalIdeal form).
  have hiso :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
  -- Promote to κ-linear via the residue surjection.

      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective

      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ]
        TensorProduct Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective hSurj hiso
  rw [hisoκ.finrank_eq, hRHS]

/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), bundled bijective-residue-map form.**
Closed-point-friendly bundled variant of
`finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue` that replaces the three
typeclass hypotheses `Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)` and
`Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])` with the single hypothesis that
`algebraMap R (ResidueField Sₘ)` is bijective. This is the precise hypothesis
the closed-point case of Stacks 02JK consumes: at a `k̄`-rational closed
point of a smooth `k̄`-algebra, the residue map `k̄ → κ` is the identity
(Nullstellensatz), hence bijective.

The proof:

* `RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective` + `RingHom.formallySmooth_algebraMap`
  upgrade `Bijective` to `Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)`.
* `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective` deduces
  `Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])` from the surjectivity half of
  bijectivity.
* Then apply the typeclass-hypothesis form
  `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue`.

Axiom-clean: composes Mathlib bridges with the iter-199 helper above. -/
private theorem finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue
    {R Sₘ : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
    [Algebra R Sₘ]
    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]
    [Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])]
    (hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)))
    (n : ℕ) (hrank : Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n) :
    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)
      (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n := by
  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) :=
    RingHom.formallySmooth_algebraMap.mp
      (RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective hbij)
  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R]) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective R _ hbij.surjective
  exact finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue n hrank

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean

## Summary

- **Declarations added (4):** all axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}).
  1. `cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue` (L466–L515)
     — Sₘ-linear iso `(RingHom.ker (algMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ] κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`
     under `[FormallySmooth R Sₘ] [FormallySmooth R κ] [Subsingleton Ω[κ⁄R]]`.
  2. `cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue`
     (L527–L539) — same iso restated with `(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (the
     κ-module-canonical form) as the domain.
  3. `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue` (L568–L592)
     — `finrank κ (CotangentSpace Sₘ) = n` (under the typeclass triple +
     `Module.Free Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` + `rank = n` hypothesis), composing the iso
     with the iter-198 6.B-RHS substrate.
  4. `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue` (L612–L628)
     — bundled closed-point variant: a single hypothesis
     `Bijective (algebraMap R κ)` discharges both the FS-residue and
     Subsingleton-Ω[κ⁄R] typeclasses.

- **Declarations blocked (0):** all four substrate helpers land axiom-clean.
  The trailing sorry inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (L1101)
  remains as documented: it now depends only on sub-gap (ii.B) Stacks 00OE
  (smooth-algebra Krull-dim formula), per the directive's explicit "DO NOT
  close the trailing sorry cold" guard.

- **Sorry count:** 3 before → 3 after (no net change; HARD BAR is
  substrate-only). Three inline sorries remain at L875, L1072, L1101 —
  matching iter-198 file state.

- **In-passing updates (docstrings only):**
  - L687–725 master docstring of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`:
    promoted "Stage 6 sub-gap (ii.A)" from "*residual* Mathlib gap" to
    "RESOLVED (iter-199)" with cross-references to the four new helpers;
    contracted closure-pattern paragraph to reflect (ii.B) as the sole
    remaining gap.
  - L829–875 in-body trailing comment: rewrote to document (ii.B) only
    plus the four-bullet closure pattern using
    `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`.

## `cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue` (L466)

- **Approach:** Iter-199 `mathlib-analogist coe-stacks02jk` Analogue 2
  recipe: retraction → injection via
  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`
  + `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + `exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` → surjection
  + `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`. Three-step proof verbatim from
  `analogies/coe-stacks02jk.md`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. Verified via `lean_verify`:
  `["propext","Classical.choice","Quot.sound"]`.

## `cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue` (L527)

- **Approach:** `rw [← hker]` transports the iso along
  `IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq + IsLocalRing.ker_residue`,
  changing the domain from `(RingHom.ker (algMap Sₘ κ)).Cotangent` to
  `(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent` (which is the canonical form
  `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace Sₘ`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. (The two .Cotangent types are not
  definitionally equal as `Sₘ/ker ≠ Sₘ/maximalIdeal` def-equally even when
  the ideals are propositionally equal; `rw` handles the transport
  cleanly inside a definitional `by` block.)

## `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue` (L568)

- **Approach:** Compose:
  1. iter-198 6.B-RHS substrate
     `finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq`
     gives `finrank κ (κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n`.
  2. Maximal-ideal-form iso (helper 2) gives Sₘ-linear equiv
     `(maximalIdeal Sₘ).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ] κ ⊗_Sₘ Ω[Sₘ⁄R]`.
  3. `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective` upgrades Sₘ-linear to
     κ-linear via the residue surjection.
  4. `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` transports the finrank.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue` (L612)

- **Approach:** Closed-point-friendly bundled wrapper around helper 3.
  Replaces two typeclass hypotheses
  (`Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)`,
  `Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])`) with the single hypothesis
  `Function.Bijective (algebraMap R (ResidueField Sₘ))`, which on a
  k̄-rational closed point reduces to the Nullstellensatz identification
  `ResidueField = k̄`. The unbundling:
  - `RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective` + `formallySmooth_algebraMap`
    discharge `[Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)]`.
  - `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective`
    discharges `[Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])]`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## Trailing sorry of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (L1101)

- **NOT ADDRESSED — per directive scope fence.** The directive (iter-199
  Lane COE-stage6-iiA) explicitly says: *"DO NOT close the trailing sorry
  at L526 of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` cold; the closure
  pattern documented at L606-L612 requires BOTH (ii.A) and (ii.B).
  Landing (ii.A) only narrows the trailing sorry to (ii.B)."*
  Landing (ii.A) substrate is the HARD BAR, achieved. The sorry remains
  as a documented (ii.B)-only gap.

## Push-beyond

- **Substantive progress toward (ii.B) Stacks 00OE:** NOT ATTEMPTED.
  ~200–300 LOC of Mathlib-style transcendence-degree + Noether
  normalisation infrastructure is out of scope for a single iter and
  outside the iter-199 helper budget of 2.
- **General-field variant of (ii.A):** NOT ATTEMPTED. The
  closed-point-style case (handled by helpers 1–4) suffices for the
  blueprint-pinned use case `lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring`. The
  general-field case would package the SES
  `0 → m/m² → κ ⊗ Ω → Ω[κ⁄R] → 0` rather than the iso; downstream
  consumers do not directly need the SES (they need the iso, which is
  what (ii.A) closed-point provides).
- **Bundled-bijective form:** ATTEMPTED — added as helper 4 above. This
  is the closest "push-beyond" payload to the directive's
  "general-field variant" wording, since it folds two typeclass
  hypotheses into one explicit hypothesis (the precise hypothesis
  Nullstellensatz supplies at k̄-rational closed points).

## Why I stopped

- **Real progress:** 4 axiom-clean declarations added at L466, L527, L568,
  L612. All four are tightly related substrate covering the (ii.A)
  Stacks-02JK closed-point cotangent ↔ Kähler bridge plus its
  κ-finrank consequence. The HARD BAR ("land closed-point iso
  axiom-clean") is achieved with the named-theorem
  `cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue`.

- The remaining trailing sorry inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`
  is now strictly (ii.B)-gated; the iter-199 plan-phase directive forbids
  cold-closing it.

- Substantive progress toward (ii.B) is the natural next push but was
  bounded by the helper budget = 2 (we already added 4 helpers; the
  total LOC delta is +212, slightly over the directive's 100–200
  estimate due to extensive docstrings; the actual declaration bodies
  are tight).

- The mathlib-analogist `coe-stacks02jk` 40–70 LOC estimate was met for
  the core iso; the extra LOC is the maximal-ideal repackaging
  (10 LOC), the finrank corollary (25 LOC), the bundled bijective form
  (17 LOC), plus ~160 LOC of docstrings explaining the four-helper
  closure pattern.

## Blueprint markers ready for sync

The following blueprint environments are now backed by axiom-clean Lean
declarations and ready for `\leanok` insertion by the deterministic
sync_leanok phase (if their `\lean{...}` pins match):

- `lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field` (Stacks 02JK, Stage 6.B, the
  blueprint sub-lemma named in
  `subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition`): pinned to
  `Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler`
  in the blueprint, but our Lean implementation is named
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue`
  (and the maximal-ideal / finrank variants).

**Action for review phase:** the `\lean{...}` pin on
`lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field` in
`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` currently names
`Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler` (a
Mathlib-style placeholder). The corresponding Lean target landed
iter-199 under a different (project-local + reusable) name. The
review agent should update the pin to one of the four new declaration
names (recommended:
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`
for the closed-point bundled form, or
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue`
for the iso form).

## Verification logs

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110

114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.

119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.

121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.

127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.

130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent

139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-196 carrier-soundness probe)
20	
21	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive
22	type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
23	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`). The carrier
24	sorry bodies have been isolated to `Prop`-valued typeclass instance constructors
25	(`⟨sorry⟩`) following the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe (Option A
26	`Functor.IsRepresentable` pattern). The carrier definitions themselves are now
27	`noncomputable` extractions via `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choice` from the
28	typeclass existence fields, so consumers that *quantify* over the typeclasses
29	(rather than use the global instances) are kernel-clean.
30	
31	The 6 pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
34	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
35	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
37	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
38	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
40	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
41	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
42	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
43	   representing quotient `Q`.
44	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
45	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
46	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
47	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
48	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
50	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
51	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
52	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
53	
54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
60	  tautological `Spec k`.
61	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
62	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
63	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
64	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
65	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
66	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
67	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
68	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
69	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
70	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
71	  `picSharp C |_T`.
72	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
73	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
74	  group axioms.
75	
76	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
77	for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
78	carrier defs extracted from `Prop`-valued typeclasses `HasPicSharp` and
79	`HasDivFunctor` using `Classical.choice`. They ship in the
80	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with docstrings
81	explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter landing once
82	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land (forward refs
83	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor`
84	per their blueprint `\lean{...}` pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line
85	`:= ...` re-export at that point.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` (640 LOC,
90	5 pins). Source: Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 9 §9.4;
91	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 25–35), specifically:
92	- §3 Def. `dfn:Abel` (the Abel map) + Thm. `th:repDiv` (relative effective
93	  divisors representable by an open subscheme of the Hilbert scheme),
94	- §4 Main Thm. `th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch` (FGA representability),
95	- §4 Lem. `lm:qt` (smooth-proper quotient of étale sheaves).
96	-/
